entries.snippet: Editorial mission descriptions across the library are short syntheses paraphrased from each mission's primary source: NASA / ESA / JAXA / ISRO / CNSA / Roscosmos / MBRSC press kits and the corresponding Wikipedia mission articles. Per-mission entries below capture the dominant primary source per mission.
entries.license_rationale: Wikipedia text is licensed under Creative Commons CC BY-SA 4.0; agency press-kit text is U.S./IGO public domain or PD-equivalent.

entries.snippet: Apollo 11 was the first crewed mission to land on the Moon. Paraphrased from NASA's Apollo 11 mission overview and the Wikipedia article on Apollo 11.
entries.license_rationale: NASA mission narratives are works of the U.S. Government, in the public domain (17 U.S.C. § 105).

entries.snippet: Curiosity (Mars Science Laboratory) is a car-sized rover exploring Gale crater. Paraphrased from JPL's Mars 2020 / MSL mission overview and the corresponding Wikipedia article.
entries.license_rationale: NASA / JPL mission descriptions are U.S. Government works in the public domain.

entries.snippet: Mars Express is ESA's first planetary mission, in orbit at Mars since December 2003. Paraphrased from ESA's Mars Express mission page and the Wikipedia article.
entries.license_rationale: ESA mission text typically released under Creative Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO.

entries.snippet: Hope (Al-Amal) is the United Arab Emirates' first interplanetary mission, in orbit at Mars since February 2021. Paraphrased from MBRSC's mission page and the Wikipedia Emirates Mars Mission article.
entries.license_rationale: MBRSC mission narratives are released for editorial reuse with attribution.

entries.snippet: Chandrayaan-3 is ISRO's third lunar exploration mission, the first to soft-land near the lunar south pole (August 2023). Paraphrased from ISRO's mission page and the Wikipedia article.
entries.license_rationale: Government of India mission text reused under permissive editorial terms (GODL-India).

entries.snippet: Planet overview copy paraphrased from NASA solar-system fact sheets and the corresponding Wikipedia articles. Numerical figures (orbital periods, masses, etc.) come from the IAU and NASA's solar-system fact sheets, which are factual and uncopyrightable.
entries.source_publisher: NASA Goddard Space Flight Center / Wikipedia contributors
entries.license_rationale: NASA fact sheets are U.S. Government works in the public domain. Wikipedia paraphrase is CC BY-SA 4.0.

entries.snippet: Sun overview copy paraphrased from NASA's Solar Dynamics Observatory and Heliophysics Division materials and the Wikipedia article on the Sun.
entries.license_rationale: NASA Heliophysics narratives are U.S. Government works in the public domain.

entries.snippet: Dwarf-planet, asteroid, and comet overview copy paraphrased from JPL's Small-Body Database, NASA Solar System Exploration entries, and the corresponding Wikipedia articles.
entries.source_publisher: NASA / JPL Solar System Dynamics group
entries.license_rationale: JPL small-body entries are U.S. Government works in the public domain.

entries.snippet: Lunar landing-site descriptions paraphrased from NASA Apollo mission press kits, the LROC site inventory, and the Wikipedia entries for each site.
entries.license_rationale: NASA Apollo press-kit text and LROC site descriptions are U.S. Government works in the public domain.

entries.snippet: Earth-object overview copy (ISS, Hubble, JWST, GPS, BeiDou, Galileo, GLONASS, Tiangong, Chandra, XMM-Newton, Gaia) paraphrased from each spacecraft's operating-agency mission page and the corresponding Wikipedia article.
entries.source_publisher: NASA / ESA / Roscosmos / JAXA / CSA / CNSA / EU GSA
entries.license_rationale: Mixed agency text — NASA portions are U.S. Government public domain; ESA portions are CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO; agency partner copy reused for editorial purposes with credit.

entries.snippet: ISS module descriptions paraphrased from NASA's ISS structural fact sheets, ESA Columbus material, JAXA Kibo material, and the corresponding Wikipedia articles.
entries.license_rationale: NASA ISS structural fact sheets are U.S. Government works in the public domain.

entries.snippet: Launch-vehicle reference copy paraphrased from each manufacturer's published mission planner / data sheet and the corresponding Wikipedia article.
entries.license_rationale: Wikipedia text is CC BY-SA 4.0; manufacturer data-sheet figures are factual and uncopyrightable.

entries.snippet: All chrome / HUD / panel / nav UI strings are original, written for Orrery. Non-English locales are translated from en-US.
entries.license_rationale: Original prose by the Orrery maintainers, MIT-licensed alongside the source code (see LICENSE).

entries.snippet: Acknowledgement copy on the /credits page is original to Orrery, written by the project maintainers.
entries.license_rationale: Original prose by the Orrery maintainers, MIT-licensed alongside the source code.
